TranslationContext: This file contains Unit Names for the Dead Nations Faction.

Translations.Comment: Risen undead kingdoms. Direct translation capturing dark, regal tone of ancient nations returned from grave.
Basic undead infantry. Direct translation; emphasizes skeletal warriors with martial discipline.
Cognate term recognized across languages. Shambling undead hordes with primal, chaotic nature.
Elite tomb guardians. Used 'Spettrale' to convey supernatural wight nature, parallel to Spanish 'Espectral'.
Disciplined spear formations. Direct translation; compound shows skeletal warriors forming ordered undead lines.
Ancient siege weapon exhumed. 'Disseppellita' (unearthed/exhumed) captures gothic imagery of risen war machines.
Silent archers. Composite noun showing skeletal precision and supernatural silence.
Cursed supernatural beasts. 'Lugubri' conveys darkness and dread, parallel to Spanish 'Sombríos'.
Undead cavalry from noble houses. Maintains knightly bearing and tactical discipline even in death.
Elite undead cavalry. Their presence radiates supernatural dread; maintains noble cavalry aesthetic.
Monstrous flying creatures. 'Orribili' (dreadful/horrible) emphasizes supernatural horror of the undead beasts.
Massive reanimated corpse. Direct compound showing terrible size and undead nature.
Skeletal undead wyrm. Evokes primal dread of bone draconic horror rising from ancient tombs.
Ancient undead noble commander. 'Spettrale' conveys otherworldly power and supreme authority.
Blood-drinking undead knight. Combines martial prowess with vampiric sorcery and immortal hunger.
Master of death magic. Standard Italian fantasy term; commands undead legions through dark sorcery.
